Q4: investigates latent reasoning formation via truncation strategy, but no fixed/canonical vector construction or intervention. Provides background on multilingual latent reasoning mechanisms that bound claims about latent reasoning.
Q1: moves activations/states across depth-recurrent steps. Q4: operates on reasoning at depth granularity in latent/hidden space via attention mixtures. Not fixed—learned architecture.

Q1: moves continuous latent reasoning states across VAE encoder/decoder boundary. Q5: uses visual-semantic regularization to label/canonicalise latent posterior distribution. Not fully fixed (learned VAE).

Q4: analyzes latent reasoning trajectories and search structure in continuous hidden space via probing beliefs at each step. Provides interpretability background on how latent reasoning operates but does not construct or verify fixed canonical representations.

Q1: moves learned conditional perturbation distributions over latent states. Q2: perturbations are not fixed—GTS learns the sampling distribution end-to-end. Neighbour mechanism (learned latent sampling policy).

Q4: analyzes internal mechanisms of latent reasoning methods at representation level. Provides interpretability background on latent reasoning behaviour and shortcuts but does not construct or intervene on fixed canonical vectors.

Q1: moves continuous visual latents (premise-conditioned) across decomposition boundary. Q2: not fixed—learned with three-stage training and Spherical Gaussian policy. Latent reasoning for multimodal case.

Q1: moves soft embeddings (probability-weighted token mixtures) at selected steps. Q2: not fixed—soft embeddings are learned. Q3: entropy-gated mechanism constrains which steps use latent vs. discrete decoding (stepwise constraint). Training-free framework.

Q2: introduces fixed/deterministic abstract token vocabulary as a learned codebook bottleneck for latent reasoning. Q5: labels/canonicalises reasoning via reserved abstract vocabulary with emergent structure. Fixed after warm-up; constrained decoding during inference.

Q1: moves latent reasoning states across RL optimization loop. Q2: not fixed—learned via GRPO policy optimization. Q4: operates at latent representation granularity. Same mechanism family as latent RL but addresses stability.

Q1: moves unified visual latent tokens (rendered reasoning+images compressed together). Q2: not fixed—learned compression. Q4: operates on unified visual-textual reasoning at latent granularity. Multimodal extension of latent reasoning.

Q1: moves memory blocks (fixed sequences of special tokens) through forward passes. Q2: memory blocks are fixed structure, but their use/semantics learned via two-stage curriculum. Q4: operates at memory-block granularity in latent space.

Q1: moves latent reasoning periods (continuous representations) between acoustic feature regions. Q2: not fixed—learned alignment of CoT supervision. Same latent reasoning mechanism applied to speech domain.

Q2: uses structural/causal/geometric probes to identify fixed geometric and semantic priors in latent vectors, operationalizes them as training-free decode-time interventions. Q5: labels/canonicalises latent reasoning via interpretability-guided priors. Direct intervention on frozen model.

Q1: moves continuous latent vectors (embedding-space trajectories) across model boundary; Q4: operates at sentence granularity in latent space via geometric path approximation; mechanism family differs from fixed/canonical vectors (learned trajectory head), so scores 2 not 3.

Q1: moves continuous latent thought vectors (normalizing-flow states) at intermediate positions; Q4: continuous latent reasoning in compact states; learned normalizing flow (not fixed/canonical), so scores 2 not 3.

Q1: moves discrete boundary tokens (<swi>/<swi>) and latent hidden-state recurrence across reasoning boundary; Q3: discrete entry/exit anchors enable mechanistic analysis and on-policy RL; mechanism is learned recurrence with discrete anchors rather than fixed structural vectors, scores 2.

Q1: moves discrete latent tokens (rendered to images, clustered into fixed vocabulary) across model boundary; Q2: discrete latent vocabulary is deterministic/canonical once constructed; Q5: explicitly constructs and aligns discrete latent tokens via clustering-based vocabulary, enabling standard autoregressive modeling.

Q3: applies counterfactual edits and noise-ablation activation patches to verify faithfulness of latent reasoning steps stepwise; Q4: analyzes causal contribution at activation level across training; negative result on faithfulness (papers explicitly verify whether steps drive answers), scores as positive mechanism would.
